conjecture_title: Open case: Coarse Menger Conjecture for c=2
paper_title: Asymptotic structure. IV. A counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture
summary: The Coarse Menger Conjecture for c=2 was explicitly left open in the source paper, which disproves the weak coarse Menger conjecture for c≥3. Subsequent work (arXiv:2509.08762) proves the coarse Menger conjecture for all graphs of bounded path-width, giving a partial positive result that applies to c=2 in that restricted class. A further paper (arXiv:2605.11112) establishes a coarse Menger theorem for planar and bounded-genus graphs. The c=2 case for general graphs remains open.

since_posted.title: Asymptotic structure. V. The coarse Menger conjecture in bounded path-width
since_posted.authors: Tung Nguyen, Alex Scott, Paul Seymour
since_posted.claim: Proves the coarse Menger conjecture for all graphs of bounded path-width (across all values of c), providing a partial positive result for the c=2 open case in this restricted graph class.

since_posted.title: A coarse Menger's Theorem for planar and bounded genus graphs
since_posted.claim: Proves a coarse version of Menger's theorem for planar and bounded-genus graphs, giving partial progress on the coarse Menger conjecture (including the c=2 case) for those surface-embeddable graph families.

internal_refs_verified.note: This paper ('Erdős–Pósa property of cycles that are far apart' by Dujmović, Joret, Micek, Morin) concerns the Erdős–Pósa property for cycles at distance >d, not the Coarse Menger Conjecture for c=2. The internal reference's contribution description is incorrect for this paper; low fuzz score (69) reflects a false match.

search_queries: Coarse Menger Conjecture c=2 paths distance graph theory 2025 2026
notes: The c=2 case of the Coarse Menger Conjecture remains open for general graphs. The source paper's main contribution is a counterexample for c≥3. Partial results exist for bounded path-width (arXiv:2509.08762) and planar/bounded-genus graphs (arXiv:2605.11112). The internal reference arXiv:2412.13893 is a false match and does not concern this conjecture.
